tossups.question: In one section of this book, the speaker imagines a conversation with four ladies who witnessed him mourning over the death of his beloved’s father. In another section of this book, the speaker dreams that he is informed of his “discourtesy” by a “young man dressed in white.” The speaker of a poem in this collection sees two (+) pretty women walking toward him and is told by “Amor” that “the first is named Primavera.” In another poem from this collection, the speaker witness the personification of Love feed his “burning heart” to a woman wearing a crimson cloth. Near the beginning of this (*) annotated prosimetrum, the speaker recalls meeting an eight year old girl before he turned ten. This collection includes a sonetto to Guido Cavalcanti, and it helped establish the Tuscan dialect as the basis for the Italian language. For 10 points, name this autobiographical collection of prose and poetry that expresses the courtly love Beatrice instilled into its author, Dante Alighieri.
tossups.answer: La Vita Nuova [or The New Life; accept Vita Nova]
tossups.answer_sanitized: La Vita Nuova [or The New Life; accept Vita Nova]

This collection includes a sonetto to Guido Cavalcanti, and it helped establish the Tuscan dialect as the basis for the Italian language. For 10 points, name this autobiographical collection of prose and poetry that expresses the courtly love Beatrice instilled into its author, Dante Alighieri.

tossups.question: In a poem with this title, the speaker is told, “The summer boils away” by a “legless beggar.” The narrator of a work with this title calls on “[his] lady Pity” to defend himself in a “maze” of conflicting thoughts. In a novel with this title, agents fighting the Great Conspiracy report to a pistol-collecting doctor who orders the murder of a cowboy comic author. A work with this title uses a poem divided into speaking, weeping and finding disconsolate women to mourn a death presaged by a vision of angels singing “Hosanna.” In a novel with this title, Uncle (*) Rifkı’s nephew quits engineering to take bus trips pursuing the angelic Janan after his life is changed by reading a book. The numerology of nine punctuates a prosimetrum with this title that includes a sonetto to Guido Cavalcanti. For 10 points, give this title of an Orhan Pamuk novel about Osman’s obsession and an annotated collection of canzoni expressing Dante’s courtly love for Beatrice.
tossups.answer: The New Life [accept La vita nuova or Yeni Hayat] (The leadin is from “La Vita Nuova” by Weldon Kees.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: The New Life [accept La vita nuova or Yeni Hayat] (The leadin is from "La Vita Nuova" by Weldon Kees.)

tossups.question: A character in this book delivers to the speaker the blistering insult, "I am like the center of a circle, equidistant from all points on the circumference; you, however, are not." The speaker of a poem in this collection closes his eyes and pictures the floating faces of women crying "You will die! You will die!" Yeats titled his poem "Ego Dominus Tuus" after words spoken to this book's speaker in a dream. The personification of Love literally feeds a lady the speaker's burning heart in a sonnet in this collection addressed "to every captive soul and gentle heart." This book describes how the narrator was nine when he met a girl who was eight, and is unable to complete the final (*) canzone when that woman suddenly dies. This book begins by describing the narrator's "book of memory," which contains a heading reading "here begins the [title thing]." This book, which consists of poems sandwiched by prose autobiographical glosses, helped establish the Tuscan dialect as the standard for the Italian language. For 10 points, name this collection by Dante inspired by his love for Beatrice.
tossups.answer_sanitized: La Vita Nuova [or The New Life]

bonuses.leadin: Walter Pater wrote that this poet achieved a “sweetness” out of strength by outliving the Renaissance. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this poet of many sonnets to Vittoria Colonna. This poet also addressed a homoerotic sonnet sequence to the much younger nobleman Tommaso dei Cavalieri.
Pater contrasted Michelangelo’s mellowed love for the widow Vittoria Colonna with this poet’s passionate depiction of Beatrice, who guides this poet in the Divine Comedy.

Dante describes his first sight of Beatrice in a collection titled for a “new” one of these things. This is the last word of almost every English translation of a poem beginning “we cannot know his legendary head.”
answers_sanitized: Michelangelo [or Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni]
life [or vita; or Leben; accept La Vita Nuova; accept The New Life]

bonuses.leadin: Ka and İpek see the director of the Institute of Education being murdered at a pastry shop of this name in the novel Snow. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this name. In a 1994 novel with this title by the same Turkish author, Osman becomes obsessed with a fictional book that has this title after Janan puts it down on his table.
A reference to the earlier novel The New Life is among the many intertextual features of the novel Snow, which is by this Turkish author of My Name is Red.

While narrating part of Snow, the character Orhan Pamuk describes writing this real-life Pamuk novel about Kemal's love for Füsun (FUR-sun). Pamuk established a real-world equivalent of this novel's title place, which displays 4,213 cigarette butts.
answers: The New Life [or Yeni Hayat; accept New Life Pastry Shop]
answers_sanitized: The New Life [or Yeni Hayat; accept New Life Pastry Shop]
The Museum of Innocence [or Masumiyet Muzesi]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Ka and Ipek see the director of the Institute of Education being murdered at a pastry shop of this name in the novel Snow. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Give this name. In a 1994 novel with this title by the same Turkish author, Osman becomes obsessed with a fictional book that has this title after Janan puts it down on his table.
A reference to the earlier novel The New Life is among the many intertextual features of the novel Snow, which is by this Turkish author of My Name is Red.

While narrating part of Snow, the character Orhan Pamuk describes writing this real-life Pamuk novel about Kemal's love for Fusun (FUR-sun). Pamuk established a real-world equivalent of this novel's title place, which displays 4,213 cigarette butts.

bonuses.leadin: A female poet from this country wrote more than 300 poems, including love poems, in a collection simply titled Rime. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this home country of Gaspara Stampa. An earlier poet from this country wrote Il Canzoniere out of his love for Laura.

Petrarch helped develop this poetic style, which was influenced by troubadours and focused on divine love. This style, whose name was first coined in Dante's Purgatorio, included the writers Guido Guinizelli and Guido Cavalcanti.

In Purgatorio, Bonagiunta da Lucca praises the poem "Ladies who have understanding of love" from this Dante collection. The last canzone of this collection is unfinished due to the death of Dante's love Beatrice.
answers: dolce stil novo [accept stilnovismo; accept sweet new style; prompt on partial answers]
answers_sanitized: dolce stil novo [accept stilnovismo; accept sweet new style; prompt on partial answers]
La Vita Nuova [or The New Life]

bonuses.leadin: The forty-two chapters of this work contain only thirty poems. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this prosimetrum work, whose poems are framed by two-part commentaries by the author.
La Vita Nuova is by this author of Convivio and The Divine Comedy.
In the Inferno, Dante sees Satan in the center of Hell, with his three mouths each chewing on a famous traitor. The center mouth is chewing on Judas Iscariot; name either of the other two traitors.
answers_sanitized: La Vita Nuova [or The New Life]
